commentaries.author: Augustine of Hippo
commentaries.content: 9. "Destroy not my soul with the ungodly" (ver. 9). Destroy not then, together with them that hate Thee, my soul, which hath loved the beauty of Thy house. "And my life with the men of blood." And with them that hate their neighbour. For Thy house is beautified with the two commandments.

commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content:
Take; literally, "destroy "(Haydock) or suffer me not to be contaminated or lost. (Worthington)
Hebrew, "gather. "Protestants, or "take not away. "(Marginal note; and St. Jerome) (Haydock)
Hebrew may be more expressive, and agrees with the parable, where God orders the cockle to be gathered into bundles, to be burnt. (Berthier)
Treat me not like the wicked and murderers, who are cut off before their time. (Calmet)
David alludes to a future state, as he knew that the wicked were not always punished here. (Berthier)
O God is not in Hebrew, Septuagint, or St. Augustine. (Calmet)
But it is understood. (Haydock)

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
